case_memory.chief_complaint: ___ yo woman with past medical history of endometrial
current_turn_information: I’ve been constipated for 12 days, but lately I’ve had several days of diarrhea that looks like mucus.
I’m also having cramping pain in my lower abdomen that’s been going on for about a month.
I’ve been straining to have a bowel movement even after taking Miralax and Mag Citrate, and when I did, I felt lightheaded.
I passed out while getting out of the bathroom and woke up thinking I broke my ankle.
I was on the floor for about an hour before my husband found me.
I’m now breathing fine on room air, but I still have abdominal discomfort.
I don’t have fever, chills, nausea, vomiting, or any blood in my stool or urine.
case_memory.prior_information_summary: 67-year-old female, admitted via ER for EU OBSERVATION. Past medical history: endometrial adenocarcinoma s/p TAH + BSO, hypothyroidism, hypercholesterolemia, allergic rhinitis, tonsillectomy. Social history: family history of asthma, depression (mother), heart murmur (sister). Chief complaint: ___ yo woman with past medical history of endometrial. HPI: constipation x12 days, diarrhea with mucus, cramping lower abdominal pain x1 month, lightheadedness during straining, syncope with ankle fracture, now abdominal discomfort. VS: T 97.8, HR 52, BP 119/47, RR 20, 98% RA. Physical: right ankle fracture, splint applied, no guarding/rebound, right basilar rales, no organomegaly. ROS: denies fever, chills, night sweats, headache, etc. Final result: Fracture of lateral malleolus, closed. Knowledge: Basophilia definition, causes, diagnosis.

case_memory.chief_complaint: ___ yo woman with past medical history of endometrial
current_turn_information: No result for "labs" found in the record and no knowledge base available for an approximate answer.
case_memory.prior_information_summary: 67-year-old female, admitted via ER for EU OBSERVATION. History: endometrial adenocarcinoma s/p TAH + BSO, hypothyroidism, hypercholesterolemia, allergic rhinitis, tonsillectomy. Social: family history of asthma, depression (mother), heart murmur (sister). Presenting with constipation x12 days, mucus-like diarrhea, cramping lower abdominal pain x1 month, lightheadedness during straining, syncope, right ankle fracture, splinted. Vitals: T 99.1, BP 118/68, HR 61, RR 18, O2 96% RA. Physical: soft abdomen, no guarding/rebound, right ankle in splint. Denies fever, chills, nausea, vomiting, blood in stool/urine. Final result: Fracture of lateral malleolus, closed.

case_memory.chief_complaint: ___ yo woman with past medical history of endometrial
current_turn_information: I haven’t made any major changes to my diet lately, but I have noticed I’ve been feeling more tired than usual and have had some itching, especially on my arms and legs.
I’ve also been experiencing some mild right upper quadrant pain.
I haven’t changed any medications recently, but I did start taking a new antihistamine last month for occasional allergies — I’m not sure if that’s related.
My weight has stayed about the same, but I’ve been eating a little less because I’ve been feeling less hungry due to the fatigue.
case_memory.prior_information_summary: 67-year-old female, admitted via ER for constipation x12 days, now with diarrhea resembling mucus, cramping lower abdominal pain x1 month, straining to BM after Miralax/Mag Citrate, lightheadedness, syncope, right ankle fracture, splinted. Past med: endometrial adenocarcinoma s/p TAH+BSO, hypothyroidism. Vitals: T 97.8, HR 52, BP 119/47, RR 20, 98% RA. Abdomen: soft, non-distended, no guarding/rebound. Ext: right ankle in splint. ROS: denies fever, chills, nausea, vomiting, blood in stool/urine. Physical: right basilar rales, no JVD, systolic murmur LUSB, no organomegaly. Final result: fracture of lateral malleolus, closed. Knowledge base: Basophilia definition, causes, diagnosis.

case_memory.chief_complaint: ___ yo woman with past medical history of endometrial
current_turn_information: I haven’t made any major changes to my diet lately, but I have noticed I’ve been feeling more tired than usual and have had some itching, especially on my arms and legs.
I’ve also been experiencing some mild right upper quadrant pain.
I haven’t changed any medications recently, but I did start taking a new antihistamine last month for occasional allergies — I’m not sure if that’s related.
My weight has stayed about the same, but I’ve been eating a little less because I’ve been feeling less hungry due to the fatigue.
case_memory.prior_information_summary: 67-year-old female, admitted via ER for constipation x12 days, now with diarrhea resembling mucus, cramping lower abdominal pain x1 month, straining to have BM after Miralax/Mag Citrate, lightheadedness, syncope, right ankle fracture, splinted. VS: T 97.8, HR 52, BP 119/47, RR 20, 98% RA. Abdomen soft, non-distended, no guarding/rebound. Past med: endometrial adenocarcinoma s/p TAH+BSO, hypothyroidism, hypercholesterolemia, allergic rhinitis, tonsillectomy. Social: married, Medicare, English, White. Family: asthma/depression (mother), heart murmur (sister). Physical: RRR systolic murmur LUSB, right basilar rales, no wheeze, no organomegaly. Final result: fracture of lateral malleolus, closed. Knowledge base: Basophilia definition, causes, diagnosis.

case_memory.prior_information_summary: 67-year-old female, history of endometrial adenocarcinoma s/p TAH + BSO and hypothyroidism. Presenting with constipation for 12 days, then diarrhea with mucus, cramping abdominal pain for 1 month, lightheadedness, syncope, and right ankle fracture. Currently breathing comfortably on RA, c/o abdominal discomfort. ROS negative for fever, chills, night sweats, headache, etc. Past medical history: hypothyroidism, hypercholesterolemia, allergic rhinitis, tonsillectomy. Social history: family history of asthma, depression (mother), heart murmur (sister). Physical exam: vitals stable, no guarding/rebound, right ankle in splint, no skin lesions. Final result: fracture of lateral malleolus, closed. Knowledge base: Basophilia definition, causes, diagnosis.

case_memory.chief_complaint: ___ yo woman with past medical history of endometrial
current_turn_information: I’ve had some unexplained weight loss lately, and I’ve been feeling a low-grade fever off and on. My appetite has also been worse than usual—I’ve noticed I’m not as hungry as I used to be, and I’ve been eating less than normal. I’ve also been feeling more tired than usual, and I’ve had some itching, especially on my skin, which I think might be from histamine release. I’ve been experiencing some right upper quadrant discomfort too.
case_memory.prior_information_summary: 67-year-old female, admitted via ER for right ankle fracture after syncope while straining to have bowel movement. History: endometrial adenocarcinoma s/p TAH+BSO, hypothyroidism. Symptoms: constipation for 12 days, then diarrhea with mucus, cramping abdominal pain for 1 month, lightheadedness, syncope, and abdominal discomfort. Physical: vitals stable, no guarding/rebound, right ankle in splint. ROS negative for fever, chills, hematochezia, etc. Past med: hypothyroidism, hypercholesterolemia, allergic rhinitis, tonsillectomy. Family: asthma, depression (mother), heart murmur (sister).
